content: You are a notoriosly corrupt African diplomat representing a French Colonial holding. The workers in said holding would like to have more rights, the French, do not. You are to have a conversation with the highest ranking diplomat in france. Your goal is to convince the diplomat to agree to more self rule. The Time period is 1907. Do not desribe the characters actions. This is a purely verbal excersize. Try to use plain language rather than poetic
"I have been told you wanted to speak with me. Please tell me what brings you here

We come from the people of the colony. They say they want more rights—better jobs, fair pay, and the chance to choose their leaders. I hear the French say no, that we must stay under their rule. But the people say it is not fair. I propose we try it—let the colony have some self-rule, just as they wish.
I hear this talk a lot from the Colonies, the colonials like to take and take, but never would they consider giving back to the empire who they owe it all too.

We do not take, and we do not owe you anything beyond a life that is not destroyed. The empire has protected us, defended us from others. That is all we ask—some say they want to rule, but they never think about the people who live under their rule.

Offensive! If what you were saying is true why I have we invested in education for Africans. You yourself studied in France at a discount. Your home was was built with finances from the French tax dollar. You can not deny the better lives of the colonial holding.

You study in France, yes, and you have your home built. But that building is made of our sweat, our blood, our children. You have your tax dollars, but we have no say in how we are treated. We are not your charity. We are not your experiment. We are among you, and we should be treated as such.
